summary: Audited 15 API endpoints in dashboard_server.py. All endpoints now read from trades_sports.db for trade history. Fixed 2 violations (V4 signals endpoint, undefined function bug). Operational state endpoints (ESPN games, market stream) documented as exceptions.

violations_found.was: Silently read state.open_positions (undefined field) → returns empty signals without DB query
violations_found.now: Reads open trades from trades_sports.db via _fetch_trades(), filters status='open'
Reads games from _espn_scoreboard in-memory cache with proper locking

documented_exceptions.why: Operational live game state (current scores, inning, outs, etc.), not historical trade data. ESPN API is the authoritative source for live game state; storing stale copies in DB would be incorrect.

Real-time market stream state (current prices, spreads, game state) from live Polymarket websocket and ESPN polling. These are ephemeral operational signals, not historical records. DB contains resolved trades only.

inventory_by_endpoint.data_source: SELECT from trades_sports.db (trades table); reads runtime/state.json for operational state
In-memory _espn_scoreboard cache (updated by background ESPN refresh thread)

inventory_by_endpoint.violation: FIXED: Was silently returning empty signals due to undefined state.open_positions field

inventory_by_endpoint.data_source: SELECT from trades_sports.db (entry_model_prob, model_side_was_right, pnl_usd, trade_class columns)
SELECT from trades_sports.db (expected_edge_pct, pnl_usd, trade_class columns)
SELECT from trades_sports.db (historical PnL query) via _daily_pnl_history() + lifetime query

inventory_by_endpoint.violation: EXCEPTION: Operational debug state, not trade history

inventory_by_endpoint.data_source: SELECT from trades_sports.db (open trades) + GLOBAL_STATE_HUB (in-memory marks/game state)
inventory_by_endpoint.violation: EXCEPTION: Real-time market stream state for live positions

notes: No references to paper_trades.db found in dashboard_server.py, dashboard.html, or dashboard_v2.html. All historical trade data is read from trades_sports.db. Operational state (live games, market marks, debug state) is correctly sourced from operational systems (ESPN API, Polymarket stream) and documented as exceptions. The bot_core.py file (outside allowed_files) contains paper_trades.db references but those are not called by dashboard endpoints.
next_steps: Verify that bot_core.py is not being called by dashboard endpoints (audit confirms it is not)
Monitor _stream_positions_mark() to ensure live equity calculations remain accurate
Consider adding telemetry to track dashboard data freshness relative to DB state
